svg.text: Three questions across 2,500 years · click any card to explore
What makes an act right? · What grounds morality? · Who counts morally?

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain Socratic ethics: virtue as knowledge, the elenctic method, why no one errs willingly, and what the trial of Socrates reveals about his moral commitments')
g.text: Virtue is knowledge — to know the good is to do it.
Wrongdoing is always ignorance, never a free choice.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain Plato\'s ethics: the Form of the Good, the tripartite soul, the city-soul analogy in the Republic, and the allegory of the cave')
g.text: The Form of the Good is the highest reality.
Justice = reason ruling over spirit and appetite.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain Aristotle\'s virtue ethics: the function argument, eudaimonia, phronesis, the doctrine of the mean, and why humans are political animals')
g.text: Eudaimonia = virtuous rational activity in a full life.
Virtue is a stable disposition to hit the mean.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain Stoic ethics: eph hēmin, why virtue is the only genuine good, the role of logos in nature, Stoic emotions, and cosmopolitanism')
g.text: Virtue alone is good; all else (wealth, health) is indifferent.
Freedom = mastery of inner response. (Epictetus, Marcus Aurelius)

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain Epicurean ethics: ataraxia, aponia, kinetic vs katastematic pleasure, the taxonomy of desires, and the Epicurean argument against fear of death')
g.text: freedom from mental anxiety and bodily pain.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain Aquinas\'s natural law theory: the four-tier legal hierarchy, synderesis, the first principle of practical reason, and how he synthesizes Aristotle with Christian theology')
g.text: Natural law = rational participation in God's eternal law.
First principle: Good is to be done; evil is to be avoided.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain and compare Hobbes and Locke: the state of nature, the social contract, sovereign power vs trustee government, and natural rights')
g.text: Hobbes: morality is created by sovereign agreement.
Locke: life, liberty, property are pre-political natural rights.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain Hume\'s moral philosophy: why reason is the slave of the passions, what the is-ought gap means, and how sympathy grounds moral judgment')
g.text: Reason cannot motivate — it is the slave of the passions.
Moral judgments express sentiment, not fact (is–ought gap).

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain Kant\'s deontological ethics: the three formulations of the categorical imperative, autonomy vs heteronomy, and the kingdom of ends')
g.text: Act only on maxims you can universalize as law for all.
Treat every person as an end, never merely as a means.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain classical utilitarianism: Bentham\'s hedonic calculus, Mill\'s distinction of higher and lower pleasures, the harm principle, and the main objections')
g.text: Right action maximizes total happiness (hedonic calculus).
Mill: quality of pleasure matters, not only quantity.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain Nietzsche\'s moral philosophy: the genealogy of morals, master vs slave morality, ressentiment, the death of God, and revaluation of all values')
g.text: Our moral values are rooted in power, history, and resentment.
The death of God demands a revaluation of all values.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain Rawls\'s theory of justice: the original position, veil of ignorance, two principles of justice, the difference principle, and reflective equilibrium')
g.text: Choose principles of justice behind a veil of ignorance
about your talents, class, and conception of the good.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain MacIntyre\'s After Virtue: why modern ethics has failed, what emotivist culture means, and how recovering Aristotelian virtue through practices fixes it')
g.text: Modern moral debate is incoherent without a shared telos.
Recover Aristotelian virtue through practices and tradition.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain Singer\'s applied ethics: preference utilitarianism, animal liberation, why distance is morally irrelevant, and the philosophical basis of effective altruism')
g.text: Sentience, not species membership, grounds moral status.
Distance does not reduce obligation to help.

svg.text: What kind of person should I be?
← from X = direct philosophical inheritance or response
